verses:
vande kṛṣṇaṁ nanda⋅kumāram
nanda⋅kumāraṁ navanīta⋅cauram

muni⋅jana⋅lobhaṁ mohana⋅rūpam
muralī⋅lolaṁ madana⋅gopālam

śrīdharaṇīṣam jagadādhāram
veṇu⋅vilolaṁ vedānta⋅sāram

upātta⋅kavalaṁ parāga⋅śavalam
vande kṛṣṇaṁ nanda⋅kumāram
en_translation:
I worship Kṛṣṇa, Nanda's son. Nanda's son, the butter-thief.

Desired by the sages, of enchanting form. The one who delights in the flute, Madanagopāla.

The Lord of Śrī (Lakshmi) and Dharaṇī (Earth), the support of the universe. The one who delights in the flute, the essence of Vedānta.

Holding a morsel of food, smeared with dust. I worship Kṛṣṇa, Nanda's son.
